dc_self_support.note: D.C. Code 16-916.01(g): below the Self-Support Reserve (133% of the one-person federal poverty guideline, updated biennially by the Mayor -- approximately $1,650/month as of the most recent update), the paying parent's obligation is individualized by the court rather than computed from the schedule, with a presumption they can pay $75/month while meeting subsistence needs (rebuttable to $0 or higher). D.C. Code 16-916.01(n): the total child support obligation, including add-ons, may never exceed 35% of the paying parent's adjusted gross income.

deviation_note: Income figures in this calculator are ANNUAL (D.C.'s schedule and worksheet operate in yearly dollars, unlike most states) -- the result is converted to a monthly figure for display. This calculator does not model the separate 'modified self-support reserve' comparison (D.C. Code 16-916.01(g-2), an additional ability-to-pay tier with its own reserve figure and a maximum-ability-to-pay calculation) -- it applies only the standard Self-Support Reserve and presumptive minimum. It also does not model Shared Physical Custody (Worksheet B, when both parents have substantial parenting time) or Split Custody. Above $240,000/year combined adjusted gross income, D.C.'s guideline does not apply presumptively -- courts determine the amount case-by-case.
